title: Enhancing repository-level code completion with selective retrieval
summary: This blog post presents Repoformer, a method for repository-level code completion that uses a self-supervised, selective retrieval strategy. The approach fine-tunes a large language model to decide whether retrieving cross-file context from the code repository will improve the generated code. The authors found that retrieval is unnecessary 80% of the time, so their selective method significantly speeds up inference by 70% while maintaining higher accuracy than a model that always performs retrieval. The technique uses a dataset automatically constructed by masking and reconstructing code to teach the model when retrieval is beneficial. Repoformer is designed as a plug-and-play policy model to improve the latency and robustness of other code generation models.
author: Di Wu
